I am extremely impressed with my GoodOmen five burner gas cooktop. I hadn't heard of this brand before, but it performs just as well as the top-brand names. The installation was easy and the delivery was fast. The price was reasonable and the product is great. The gauge of metal is on the thin side, but for the price it does the job. The tray is slightly deeper than most, which makes it easy to clean and keeps boiled over liquids from draining into the elements and running underneath. The appearance of the unit is great and the actual metal knobs are a nice touch. However, the lettering is lightly printed on instead of being permanently laser etched and it was fading the first time I had to give it a deep cleaning. Overall, it's a great cooktop that is well-built, it's a nice addition to outdoor cooking as well to save heating up the house during the summer. Original:

Love some of the engineering behind it.

The ice sits in a tray and if it melts while it waits to be used it drips back into the water reservoir to be made back into ice.

My husband and I had the opportunity to purchase a 1/2 cow with some friends from a farm in TN. It was a dream of mine for decades and we finally were able to purchase not only healthier meat, but much cheaper than grocery store.  We purchased a chest freezer and the meat came about a month ago. We are happy with our investment. Then our friend who we purchased the cow with suggested we purchase a Yolink to watch the temperature of our freezer in case of a power outage while we're away or the freezer stopped working since theres now over 1k of meat in our freezer. I didn't think it was necessary, but I figured why not. I'm glad I did! I purchased this package, because it's cheaper than buying them separate and I need the hub. I also figured, I'll throw one in our fridge freezer because why not. Step was really easy and the instructions were easy to follow. Installation maybe took about 10-15 minutes, probably not even. The app is easy to set up. You can change the names of the each sensor so you know where it's located and set alerts. You don't need to open the app to have it alert you that there's an issue. That was a big issue for me with other brands. I'm not going to open the app every hour to make sure everything is working fine. You can set it to send you emails, texts or app alerts. On the 2 day after placing all the temp sensors, my phone started going off at 11pm every 10 minutes. At first, I thought it was a friend texting or a email, but it kept going off. I finally checked my phone, I was receiving alerts that my kitchen freezer us really warm. I got up and saw that my freezer wasn't closed all the way. The Yolink saved me hundreds of dollars in meat! I'm so glad I purchased these. I will now be replacing my water sensors with the Yolink brand. I recommend these to everyone.

This brush was exactly what I needed to clean my dryer vent, which has a couple of right angle bends in the ductwork. The fairly flexible shaft may seem like its a bit "wet noodle-y", but that's what makes it easier to work it around bends without undue pressure, which can risk damage to the ductwork. I found that it just takes is a bit of twisting on the handle with one hand while pushing the shaft with the other to move it through the bends. I could have used a couple more feet of shaft length to get the brush through in one pass, but it was easy enough to just make one pass from the dryer end of the duct, then another from the outside end, then blow any remaining, loosened lint out with a low-power blast from an electric leaf blower. I won't claim the the job is something I'll be looking forward to doing once a month because it's so much fun, but this brush made it a lot easier, and at least I won't dread doing it whenever needed again in the future.

I'm a reasonably competent homeowner who has repaired a dishwasher and replaced another dryer's heating coil before. This was a more difficult operation than either of those for my particular dryer model. You should definitely look up video instructions for how to replace the belt and rollers for your model.

The kit itself seems to be of high quality and our dryer went from "loud and disturbing" to "just above a whisper" after installing these parts.

The dryer brush did an excellent job cleaning the lint from the dryer ductwork. Each section screwed together easily but did not tighten down so I used duct tape to wrap each small connection point, plus over the top of the brush head to be certain that every part stayed together.  I used a cordless drill to rotate extentions brush assembly several feet throughout the long ductwork from outside the house into the laundry room. Expect a lot of lint to come out while brushing it out repeatedly for a few minutes! I disconnected the flexible aluminum vent from behind the dryer and cleaned it very well outdoors with the cordless drill, extentions and brush. You need a helper to hold one end of that flexible vent while cleaning from the other end. I discovered a tiny burn hole in the flexible aluminum vent! We are blessed that it did not cause a big fire!  We cut the flexible vent much shorter. After removing the front lint filter, the assembly did well in cleaning that duct. The extentions are somewhat flexible, but, I could not see beyond a turn inside the dryer. Last, I cleaned from the back of the dryer.  Last, hooked up the clean, shorter flexible vent to the duct and to rear of dryer. Turned on dryer to blow any remaining loose lint outside. Inspected with a flashlight and was quite satisfied with how clean the ductwork is.

It's not often a repair turns into an upgrade. After one of the nylon cords connecting my Kitchenaid dishwasher door to the counterbalancing spring broke, the door would drop precipitously and I was under orders from my boss (wife) to fix it. I found a number of videos explaining how to do the repair. They mentioned that the replacement part had two pulleys where the original one just had one and a hub the cord would ride against. I chose to replace both of the cords and pulley assemblies, though all I needed to replace was one cord.

The repair went smoothly. The only challenge was whether I could pull out the dishwasher far enough to easily reattach the spring to the frame without disconnecting all of the hoses, since it fell off when the cord snapped. I was able to pull it out just far enough to hook the back of the spring when holding the front end. I recommend first attaching the cord to the spring. It was easy to pull the spring with the cord assembly to provide slack when threading it through the pulleys and attaching to the door.

I was very pleasantly surprised when the door no longer groaned or squeaked when opened or closed. I attribute that to the 2nd pulley, since it reduces friction so nothing is rubbing, anymore. I have since ordered another set for our 2nd dishwasher to cut down on annoying noises.

I love my apartment, but I hate doing dishes. Of all the household chores that are required to keep a place looking neat, dish washing is the most tedious. As a result, dishes inevitably piled up in my sink until I was finally, reluctantly, forced to clean them by hand. Gross. And boring.

Thankfully, I received one of these lovely SPT countertop dishwashers from my brother for Christmas. Who is SPT you ask? Heck if I know. With the possible exception of Magic Chef you'd be hard-pressed to find a name brand in the small dishwasher segment. Fortunately, the only thing that matters here is that SPT has made a really, really great countertop dishwasher that is perfect for a residence with 1-2 people.

Pros:

-Setup is easy. If you can figure out how to unscrew the nozzle head from your kitchen faucet, you can set this thing up.
-Attaching and detaching the hose to the faucet is done with a single snap from the quick connector adapter.
-The internal layout is set up in such a way that it can accommodate most plates and dishes (very large platters might be a problem though).
-For a relatively large countertop appliance, it's relatively quiet.
-It uses relatively little water: only 3.8 gallons on the very heaviest cycle. To put that in perspective, this is less water than it would take to flush your toilet three times. I certainly used more water than this each time I did the dishes by hand.
-Pop some Cascade Complete tablets in the detergent dispenser and your dishes will come out sparkling, squeaky clean every time.
-The instruction manual, although bland, is helpful and well written.

Cons:

-The dishwasher is a bit heavy and bulky. A person who lacks upper body strength may struggle to set it up or move it around unaided.
-The cup rack on the upper left hand side of the plate rack can only accommodate items the height of coffee cups. Full-size drink cups will have to share space with the plates.

In short, this dishwasher literally saved me from moving to another apartment. There may be a better model out there but this one has exceeded all of my expectations.

One final word... before buying, make sure that your apartment lease allows countertop appliances. Some do, some don't.

I am updating my review for this washing machine, and now I give it all 5-Stars!! It works great!

I purchased a ""Y" Mixer Hose hose connector (as you can see in the photo) for my cold & hot water faucets.....and after I bought a 4-foot hose with 2 female ends, the washing machine works fine.  (I have that 4-foot hose connected to the "Y" Mixer hose, and also to the back of the water inlet joint on the washing machine.)

Of special note:  for the water inlet to the washing machine, itself, I had to put the little rubber washer that came with the hose of the washing machine, inside and on top of the larger washer.....of one of the ends of the 4-foot washing machine hose.  This section of that 4-foot hose is the one that screws to the washing machine water inlet.  (You can see that I have Plumber's Tape wrapped around the threaded pipe inlet joint.)

And this part of the washing machine hose that screws into the water inlet joint in the back of the washing machine has the 2 washers---one on top of the other...(on the bottom, is the larger washer that came with the 4-foot hose...and on top of that washer, I put the little washer that came with the washing machine's hose.)

(Otherwise, if you don't do that, water will leak and spray all over the place....as it first happened to me the first time I used it by using just the washer that came with the 4-foot hose----there are 2 washers, one on each end.)

Now the washing machine works great, and the water that comes inside of the tub is at the desired water temperature that I want.  

(So, from the 1-Star rating that I originally gave it, I now give it 5-Stars.) 

It washes the clothes great, and they come out really clean after the last spin cycle...and the clothes come out nearly dry and hardly wrinkled, because it takes most of the water out and the clothes are not soaking wet. Then I put the clothes in my dryer for a few minutes to dry them.

Now, after getting the "Y" Mixer Hose, and the 4-foot washing machine hose, I'm very satisfied with this little Wanai Washing Machine.  :)

I will be posting a video to accompany my review. This machine is quite the little helper. My currant situation requires me to hand wash clothes since I am now living in a two room apartment in a city. I never had to hand wash before and was always use to a washer. This machine isn't a set-and-go so much as a full size washer. It only helps with hand washing small garments and items of the sorts. The less you put in the machine, the better the results. I am quite happy with the results of the wash and the clothing really does come out cleaner than a large size washer. Perfect for the clothing of two people and I do produce allot of laundry for one person. Just takes a moment to get use to the machine and to know its limits. Once a routine is established, you are good to go. The only complaint I would have is that first of all you have to know where the max fill is. Since there is no markings, I had to fill it up till the water didn't drop from the top securement when running (about 1/4 of the way from the top of the machine or 3/4 full of water including garments) and also, the bottom of the unit gets hot from the spin cycle of its ran too long. Put a fan pointed at it and it will be okay. Can't handle too much weight in the spin basket but can wash a bit more than 6 lbs. just have it close to the water source and close to a drain. I have it on the kitchen counter, next to the sink and it just fits. If it wobbles from the spin just even out the clothing or put less in the basket. I do recommend this product to those who even just want a small washer to take on small tasks. Example, it can wash your work uniform and under garments for the following day if you are in a pinch. All the positive reviews on here are very helpful and I will be contributing a video to help you get an idea of how to use this machine.

Edit 9/8/2020: It's still great! A couple weeks ago there was a power outage and it stopped working. I was afraid it was broken because it just wouldn't make ice, but I realized it had "turned off" with the ice tray in the wrong position and didn't know how to reset itself. I just pushed it back, and it went right back to making ice :)

10/28/2019:
I bought this for my apartment because my freezer does not make ice and I do not have enough space for trays. I love it! It's been a few weeks now and everything has been going well.

I have only ever used the large sized ice, and they are not so large. People often comment about how "cute and tiny" they are. I leave it running all day because when the ice melts, it just goes right back into the water reservoir.

It's not large but it's also not small. It's roughly the size of a bread maker. I've been blessed with ample counter space, so it's not taking up valuable real estate, but for someone with a cramped kitchen it might not work. But it can go anywhere, as long as there's an outlet, it's not confined to a kitchen.

Complaints:
- One cycle is not enough ice for one person. You needs at least two or three batches of ice for a good drink. Because the ice is so small and because they're hollow, it takes more ice to cool a drink than would ice from a tray or from a freezer dispenser. As I said, I leave it on all day, so this is rarely an issue.
- If I have a lot of people over it's not so helpful. It makes more than enough for just me or me and two other people, but it would take a while to get enough ice for one pitcher.
- When the ice drops, it makes a clattering sound. It's not loud but it is clear. Had to explain that to a couple people.

Have had the dishwasher for about 1 year now; whirlpool obviously made this model to make large profit margin and fail after about 1-2 years of use. 100% in-excusable that a dishwasher in this price range would have this low of a mtbf.

Make no mistake; company as large as whirlpool knows exactly the MTBF (mean time between failures) and use the KPI vs cost before releasing the product.

1. All the plastic has turned brittle; replacing top shelf tabs and wheels is normal occurrence; about 6 months life span before you have issues.
2. Dishes come out dirty; constantly re-washing. Silverware is the worst typically.
3. You need to open dishwasher as soon as the cycle is done; leaving overnight; the dishes will develop a film as they are typically left soaked / humid from steam which stays in dishwasher until you open the door.

Bottom line; spend the extra 200 and get a bosch; life will be good for about 5 years instead of 6 months.

Apparent that whirlpool does not care about keeping customers and is applying "lean/lean" concepts to increase stockholders earnings at the price of customers.

Update; 2 years now; have spent over 500 dollars keeping the machine going. Have now replaced the top rack twice and the bottom rack wheels 3 times. Worse product I have ever purchased... Unbelievably disappointed with whirlpool.

Sent whirlpool a letter with rcpts from the first 2 years of use on keeping this pile of junk running; they basically said "we dont care" in a very nice way.

Upgrading to a Bosch this week; will have a whirlpool free house going forward.

Hands down 5/5 stars for me. Now I'm not exaggerating when I say this is the best washer I have ever had. I've lived around and used many washers but DAMN this takes the freakin cake. It's fast, easy to use, and holds even my largest blankets/comforters. Now yes it cost a pretty penny but for me it was definitely worth it. It's the fastest washer I've ever had. What usually would take me 3 days (I have a lot of people in my home) to do laundry it helps me finish in 1. I especially love all the different wash cycles it has. The men who delivered it were very sweet and gave me some tips on this model and hauled away the old one for me. (pay extra for that. TOTALLY worth it)  Tips: it can hold a LOT of laundry but still DON'T overstuff it. Also when you're in between loads leave the door open so it airs properly. When done completely with laundry leave the door open for about 15 min or so THEN close it. It will help keep away that nasty moldy smell. Overall I HIGHLY recommend this washer. It works so much better than my old one. Honestly the old one just didn't quite get the smell out of my daddies undies so that was definitely a extra star worth for me personally. IF you get this washer I hope you enjoy it as much as I do! :)

-both coarser and finer grinds than what I typically use for paper filters

My Kone arrived today with a very thin, nearly unnoticeable layer of very fine powder, almost ash-like, coating the inside surface. It was most prominent near the bottom of the funnel, where it seemed to have built up and was clearly visible. Running my finger along the inside surface built up a decent amount on my finger tip, and dabbing my finger the center of the bottom showed significant amounts (see photos attached). Most bothersome was that, after neglecting to clean it off my finger immediately after, the ash was somewhat difficult to remove from my finger tip. I assume the oils of my skin made it more "sticky", but nevertheless this isn't something I'd want in my beverage. Will definitely put this through a thorough cleaning before using.

I suggest Able Brewing Co. double check their QC procedures before more of these go out the door. There doesn't seem to be any markings on the product itself to indicate manufactured date or batch # or any sort of traceability, but for reference I ordered mine in March 2016.

The negative reviews for this product rose the following concerns:
1 - The lid doesn't easily close.
2 - The brew result is weak.
3 - No Trace of Gold on the Mesh.
4 - Not fitting in Keurig machine.
5 - Lids break off after less than a year of use.
6 - Coffee grounds in my coffee.
We've used [and re-used] these pods for several months, now, so I'm in a position to address most of these complaints:
1 - Maybe if there are coffee grounds on the lip. Otherwise, we've found, beyond the expected resistance of an o-ring seal, they close quite easily, and completely for an excellent seal. And, even if a few grains between the lid and the pod, they, typically, crush under minor pressure. Maybe the reviewers are not grinding there beans enough [or not at all]. See #2, below.
2 - Yes, our first brews resulted in weak, watery coffee. So, we made a few adjustments:
a - We switched to a Dark French Roast. But, consider that we were accustomed to drinking a Medium Roast [Starbucks Veranda], so if you like a strong cup of coffee, this might not satisfy.
Following is the coffee we now use:
> Ground: https: //amzn.to/2QAG9cv
> Whole Beans: https://amzn.to/2QAH2Sn
[strangely, the smaller 12oz bags are a better value than any of the larger quantity offers!!]
b - We always make sure the pod is filled completely to the brim.
c - We buy the whole beans, and make sure to thoroughly grind them, for the greatest surface area.
Another, possible, reason for a weaker brew is the way these pods are constructed. The geometry of the pod is dictated by a lower needle, in the Keurig machine. When I did my original research, it was clear there are two main solutions [across the various products I considered]:
a - Completely bottom out above the needle, resulting in a flat inner base.
Con: Under-utilizes the available volume.
Pro: Easier to clean.
b - Extend the bottom of the pod beyond the needle, with a raised, central "terrace" that avoids the needle.
Con: Harder to clean.
Pro: Accommodates a larger volume of coffee grounds.
I opted for easier to clean.
3 - The mesh on all 6 of our pods are quite gold.
4 - We have an old K-40 Elite machine, and the pods fit quite nicely. But, to be fair, the negatives, of this category, involved model numbers such as k150 and k200, so I have no experience, there.
5 - So far, the hinges, on our pods, are in good form -- but, our use duration likely falls short of the reviewer's "less that a year". But, there are three of us, using them once, and even twice, a day, and they seem fine. Our use is, though, spread across all 6 pods, with no accounting for frequency/pod.
6 - Maybe one time [out of hundreds], did I find a coffee ground floating in my coffee. And, never, at the bottom of the cup. This just doesn't happen. The mesh on these reusable pods is very fine, and even when filling them with coffee bean's that I've ground the snot out of [I actually shake the grinder, to get as much product into the blades, as possible], nothing escapes [except, maybe, that one spec I previously alluded to]. In fact, it's more likely the pod chamber, on the Keurig machine, needs a cleaning, as coffee dust can accumulate there. BTW: The reason I'm so zealous in my coffee grinding, is to get the grounds as fine as possible, for the greatest surface area, as remedy for issue #2.

I love this machine, I wanted to give it 5 stars but (like some of the other reviews stated) came dented which is the reason I knocked off a star. The box was undamaged and the machine seemed to packed well so I’m not sure where the dents came from. I reached out to Amazon customer service and was offered a discount, so I kept it.  I’ll be able to pop most of the dents myself. My W/D hookups are in a closet where it’s out of sight, so the dents don’t really bother me. The machine itself is GREAT. Easy to hookup and operate. I connected it to the W/D hookups in my apartment, but it can connect to any sink faucet. Quiet operation, even on the spin cycle! Gets my clothes squeaky clean and is gentle enough for my delicates. Clothes come out almost dry. This little thing is a dream come true for an apartment dweller, especially during the pandemic. I can’t believe I didn’t get one sooner. I live alone so the capacity is perfect for my needs. Can wash pretty decent sized loads. I did a lot of research on compact washing machines and this by far had the best reviews. Just unfortunate that they have dents because it’s such a cute little machine. Hopefully the manufacturer addresses the issue because it’s a really great washer.

Washer Pros: Cleans great, perfect for our small space, no maintenance issues for the last 2 years. Great on water conservation! Good capacity.
Washer Cons: Many, too many washing options. For example, why would there be a separate "extra wash" button that give you an extra 3 minutes on your wash cycle? Too many wash cycle combinations overall! Furthermore, wash cycle takes forever! Don't forget to wipe-out the extra water remaining in the rubber inside tubing after EVERY wash cycle to avoid mold. Keep washer door open after every wash cycle to allow washer to dry.

Dryer Pros: Nothing extaordinary
Dryer Cons: Automatic dryer sensor doesn't work, especially on medium and small dyer loads. I had the sensor replaced within 1 month of purchasing this machine, but it made no difference. I therefore have to override the settings to be "extra dry" or run the machine on time-set.

In short, buy these machines for their small size, good maintenance records and quiet/efficient wash cycle. If these are not priorities, choose another model!

We had just purchased a new washer and dryer set.  It is a HE washer, but still a top loader.  But it has 5.3 cubit feet capacity and is HEAVY, with intense spin cycles.  Yet despite being so heavy, it still walked during the spin cycle, and was impossible to move when full of heavy wet clothes.  Pretty hard to move even WITHOUT wet clothes.  Even hubby had trouble moving it.  So it wasn't just a matter of nudging it back in place like with the old washer.  Hubby researched and decided on this set of rubber feet.  I was aghast at the price, but we bought two packs (decided what the heck, let's put it on the dryer, too.)  Needed our brother-in-law to come help install, it's not easy with large/heavy machines.  And once installed, there's NO moving it, so make sure it's already exactly where you want placed.  So far have done three loads, including one with heavy bulky bedding, and we were pleasantly shocked to see that the washer stayed in place.  We had put taped markers in front of the feet for visual confirmation, and the washer hasn't budged at all.  We are going to continue to leave the tape on the floor to see how it looks after a month or so.

My fridge came with the house when I bought it 8 years ago. Has always worked perfectly fine besides the last 2 months. I was actually planning on buying a new fridge just because this one is pretty old, but with the supply chain so low right now it was basically impossible to find one.
At first, my fridge and freezer wouldn’t get cold enough and both freezer and fridge temperatures were very high (my fridge has digital temperatures and normally at 0 on freezer and 37 on fridge). I could hear that the compressor was kicking on and the fridge was trying to get cold. After doing some investigating and reading things online I found that the condenser fan on the outside of the fridge was not working at all and I replaced the fan which I got on Amazon for $16. After replacement, the fan was working and fridge went back to normal…for about 8 days. This time, the freezer temp went too low and the fridge temp was too high. I rechecked and the fan was still working. After doing some more investigating I found that the coil on the inside of the freezer was freezing up causing the issue. I bought this $6 sensor and replacing it was fairly easy, about 15 minutes after letting the freezer coil thaw out with some fans. It’s been over 2 weeks now and my fridge has been at a constant 37 degrees and freezer at 0 degrees. The issue is now fixed and hopefully I can still get some more life out of this fridge instead of spending $1800 on a new one.

So it certainly has more features and sensors than most devices in its class, but it's limited by a few peculiar snags. As far as motion sensors go, it has too much of a lag with my Smartthings hub to be as useful as their own motion sensors (which are also thinner). I tried setting it up to turn on my Hue bulbs when I walk into the room, but there's a delay of about 2 seconds (compared to the near-instant response of my other motion sensors).

Since it didn't work well for that purpose, I tried moving it to another role in my mailbox. Since a small delay doesn't matter as much, it seemed like a useful way to signal when mail had arrived as well as a way to provide information on humidity, temperature, etc outdoors. Unfortunately, its range is considerably less than my other sensors, so it fails to connect despite being just ~20 feet from my Wi-Fi router and SmartThings hub (though admittedly it now had to function through walls, in this application).

Also, the choice of batteries is peculiar to me. Not being an expert in such things, I assume it was the best they could do, but the fact that it doesn't run on simple AA, AAA, or 9V batteries surprised me a bit.

These ‘rack adjusters’ are the plastic pieces that attach the upper rack of your Dishwasher to the metal side rails inside the machine that allow the rack to slide in and out. They are usually the first things to break because they take a lot of stress and are (don’t ask me why) made of PLASTIC. Original parts are expensive and take time to get, so I ordered these so I could continue to use the machine while I’m waiting. Their 4 small plastic pegs with tiny flanges must fit precisely into the little metal slots in the side rails or the pieces don’t work. Theses did not, because the pegs were too thick and had slub on them from sloppy manufacturing. If you find a good instructional video online it takes 15 minutes to replace these parts. Because I had to sand them down, try them, sand them some more, try again, etc., until I could get them to fit into the slots, it took more like two hours. Hopefully my fix will hold until I get the ‘genuine factory’ replacement parts that cost $50 each, not including shipping. My dishwasher is only about 6 years old. I’m hoping the next one I buy will have metal parts.

During our kitchen redo we ran into the issue of our cooktop not being available for 5 months so we bought this to fill the gap. It works just fine if you have realistic expectations of a lightweight countertop induction cooktop.

Pros - simple and safe to use. Easy on off/adjustments, not too noisy, easy to clean, lockable. Settings are easy to read and the markings held up with no wear after 5 months use. It did everything we needed it to do from frying, cooking pasta, making soup, etc. Cleaning was a matter of wiping it off (the lock feature was nice here to help stop the beeping when wiping the controls).

Great for heating up tortillas really quickly in a dry pan! Almost like having a portable comal! :)

Controls are simple to use, although why they have multiple scales of f/c/watts seemed odd. Straight scale of 1 through 10 would make more sense as the temp settings did not really correspond to actual use. Lowest setting in Fahrenheit was 186f which would simmer a small pot alternating between rapid simmer and no simmer as the element cycled. Probably closer to 205 with the on/off cycle.

Cons - as others have mentioned it has a hard time simmering properly. There is a slightly too large gap between the lowest two setting so you either get a vigorous simmer on 2 or a simmer that can only be maintained with the lid on. Again, reasonable expectations. Elements are optimum for 4-8” pots or pans, a larger stock pot will take a while to heat up.

It does need a 20 amp circuit and will pop the circuit breaker if you have another appliance on the same one - draws almost 20 amps on its own on the two highest settings.

Overall, nice little cooktop, no regrets. Our contractor bought it once our real induction top arrived, he’s going to use it in his camper. Perfect use for it.

Just like the other reviews say, this item works as intended and is nicely made. Now I can take the eggs out without removing all the things I stacked on top of the egg carton!

2 minor things to mention:

1. The sides of the main compartment are a bit less sturdy than the rest so be careful when washing. This does not in any way impact functionality because once you put the top on, it becomes very sturdy.

2. Another reviewer mentioned that the white part is to attach it to another holder. While this is true, you also need it to tilt the holder so that the eggs roll forward, so do put the white thing on first before putting your eggs in.

UPDATE: Now that I have used this item until the last egg, I have found that the last egg in the row will either not roll forward or will actually roll all the way back to the back end of the container. Bummer.

What happens is that when there are more than 2 eggs in a row, the back egg will push the front eggs forward. When there are only 2 eggs in a row, the eggs roll forward a bit, but really just sit wherever they happen to stop. When there is 1 egg, it won't move forward or it will actually roll all the way back. I realized that this was probably due to the refrigerator being angled backward. (When you install a fridge, the front feet are raised so that the fridge leans back a bit to help the door close and to keep the fridge from rolling around.) That means they did not test this item well because they should have made up for that by angling the container more forward. Also, this issue cannot be due 100% to the refrigerator angle because then none of the eggs would roll forward to begin with. Then I noticed that the attachment that goes under the container to angle it only goes a bit more than half way. In other words, the front part of the container is not angled at all.

1-star ding for not working at 100%.

UPDATE #2: I adjusted the fridge so that it is not leaning so far back. That did the trick, so if you are also having problems, try adjusting the fridge. Just make sure that the fridge door still closes by itself (it should slam a bit) when you let the handle go. Back to 5 stars...
